revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hinooka Kofun]][[ja:日岡古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Hinooka Kofun | native_name =日岡古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Hinooka-kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Hinooka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Hinooka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.344388888889|N|130.76733333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ukiha|qid=Q850373|1=Ukiha, Fukuoka}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|20|40|N|130|46|02|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Hinooka Kofun'''|日岡古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Yoshii neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ukiha, Fukuoka|en|Ukiha, Fukuoka|qid=Q850373|lt=Ukiha|1=Ukiha, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1928.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217609|title=日岡古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> Together with the nearby Tsukioka Kofun and Tsukado Kofun, it forms the Wakamiya Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Hinooka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located within the precincts of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine along Fukuoka Prefectural Route 749, which runs through the {{ill|Alluvial plain|en|Alluvial plain|qid=Q656586|lt=Alluvial plain|1=Alluvial plain}} on the south bank of the {{ill|Chikugo River|en|Chikugo River|qid=Q954320|lt=Chikugo River|1=Chikugo River}}. It has a total length of 74 meters and a height of five meters and was surrounded by a moat. In the posterior circular portion has a diameter of 40 meters, and contains a horizontal passage stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] that opens to the southwest. The structure of the burial chamber is such that the side walls are tilted slightly inward, and on the back wall, a large monolith more than 2.2 meters wide and 1.9 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and the back wall has geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns, {{ill|Bracken |en|Bracken |qid=Q258034|lt=''warabi'' fern|1=Bracken}} patterns, and triangular patterns in three colors of red, white, and green. The surrounding walls are painted with depictions of weapons, fish, ships, horses, shields, beasts, etc. in different colors covering almost the entire surface of the burial chamber. The tumulus is estimated that it was built in the first half of the 6th century, during the late Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Related materials are on display at the Ukiha City Yoshii History and Folklore Museum. The tumulus is approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Chikugo-Yoshii Station|en|Chikugo-Yoshii Station|qid=Q4844938|lt=Chikugo-Yoshii Station|1=Chikugo-Yoshii Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|en|Kyūdai Main Line|qid=Q870569|lt=Kyūdai Main Line|1=Kyūdai Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is approximately 2.1 kilometers northeast of {{ill|Chikugo-Yoshii Station|en|Chikugo-Yoshii Station|qid=Q4844938|lt=Chikugo-Yoshii Station|1=Chikugo-Yoshii Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kyūdai Main Line|en|Kyūdai Main Line|qid=Q870569|lt=Kyūdai Main Line|1=Kyūdai Main Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|qid=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.ukiha.fukuoka.jp/kiji0035112/index.html Ukiha City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=84 Cultural properties in Fukuoka Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/12686 Fukuoka Tourism web]{{in lang|ja}}
Hinooka Kofun
